mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real corporate profit margins and trigger anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns occur in over 65% of months following CPIAUCSL increases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that forces immediate equity deleveraging and produces negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of threshold-crossing events over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts amplifies aggregate demand growth, compressing risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in over 58% of instances, invalidating the supply-contraction penalty.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 5.0 percent imposes severe financing costs on highly leveraged firms, triggering broad equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market absorption that supports consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of observations, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant at the 95% confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion phase signals impending credit contraction and reduced construction activity, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines in late-cycle regimes generate positive 5-day returns in over 62% of cases, negating the housing-led downturn signal.
